assumption_note: Apple fritters run noticeably bigger than a standard donut -- used 3.0 oz for the "small" variant.
Project owner review: 5.0 oz was too low for the "large"/regular-size fritter (apple fritters run notably bigger than a standard donut); revised to 6.0 oz.
Project owner review: 14 oz overstated the filled weight of a salad served in a 32 oz container (container is a volume capacity, not filled food weight); revised to 8 oz / 0.5 lb.
Standard bakery donut proxy at 2.5 oz each (cake, old fashioned, raised -- not the apple fritters, matched separately above).

Catch-all for the PTF/Max/7TF sandwiches, wraps, paninis, and burritos -- a 6-inch submarine-style sandwich runs roughly 7 oz per nutrition-tracking sources; applied the same estimate across torpedo rolls, baguettes, ciabatta, and wraps since none state their own size.
